issue.title: Bug: Blank-screen regression v2 — App.tsx:152 passes fresh {} to useStaleWorkDetection → infinite re-render

successCriteria.description: App.tsx uses stable empty-constant fallbacks for activeWork and recentIssues — no fresh {} / [] allocations in the render path
Desktop launches with no active project → KanbanBoard renders the empty-state UI without an infinite loop or console warnings
'Maximum update depth exceeded' does not appear in console during normal app lifecycle (open with project / open without project / switch projects)
An error boundary wraps the panel sections so a single panel crash falls back to a recoverable UI rather than unmounting the whole tree
pnpm build, pnpm typecheck, and existing pnpm test all pass

phases.title: App.tsx stable-constant fallbacks + ErrorBoundary
phases.summary: Part A (stable constants): Added module-level EMPTY_ACTIVE_WORK and EMPTY_RECENT_ISSUES constants to apps/desktop/src/App.tsx after imports. Replaced FOUR fresh-allocation sites — line 158 (activeWork fallback in useStaleWorkDetection hook arg), line 252 (setRecentIssues in default-cwd loadState branch), line 279 (setRecentIssues in project-specific loadState branch — the plan-mentioned 4th site that the diff initially missed; caught by the verification grep), line 336 (recentIssues check in pipeline-complete watcher), line 366 (setRecentIssues in file-changed listener). Part B (ErrorBoundary): created apps/desktop/src/components/ui/ErrorBoundary.tsx — class component with getDerivedStateFromError + componentDidCatch, optional fallback render-prop, optional label, default fallback shows error name+message + Reload button that resets boundary state. Added ErrorBoundary.css (1265 bytes, dark-theme tokens). Wired into apps/desktop/src/components/ui/index.ts (named + type export). Wrapped App.tsx panel-group with NESTED boundaries (center-pane outer + per-view inner: terminal-view / kanban-view / dependencies-view / settings-view) — granular fallback so one panel crash doesn't take down the others within the center pane. 5 <ErrorBoundary wraps total in App.tsx.
phases.verificationResults: PASS — all 8 verification criteria met. (1) EMPTY_ACTIVE_WORK and EMPTY_RECENT_ISSUES present at module scope (grep confirms literals). (2) recentIssues || [] count = 0 (was 2 before fix). (3) activeWork ?? {} count = 0 (was 1 before fix). (4) ErrorBoundary.tsx exists as class component with static getDerivedStateFromError AND componentDidCatch (verified by Read). (5) App.tsx imports ErrorBoundary from ./components/ui AND uses 5 <ErrorBoundary wraps (verified by grep). (6) pnpm build clean (44.02s, no errors). (7) pnpm typecheck clean (3 of 4 workspace projects ran; framework has no TS to check). (8) pnpm test: shared 65/65 (1.24s), desktop 122/122 (9.93s) — UNCHANGED from v0.6.4 baseline (no new tests added; this is a behavioral fix), framework 10/10 (1.07s). pnpm test:rust failed environmentally (Windows file-lock on tiki-desktop.exe — running app holds the binary), but cargo check --tests clean confirms Rust source compiles. No Rust source touched in #212.
phases.content:
Apply the v0.6.6 hotfix in one focused phase. Both the bug fix (stable constants) and the prevention (error boundary) are small, tightly related, and both touch App.tsx, so a single phase is cleaner than splitting.

### Part A — Stable empty constants in App.tsx

1. Add two module-level constants near the top of `apps/desktop/src/App.tsx` (after imports, before the `App` component), matching the v0.6.5 `EMPTY_TABS` / `EMPTY_COLUMN_ORDER` pattern from terminalStore.ts and kanbanStore.ts:
   ```ts
   // Stable empty-fallback constants. These prevent fresh-object allocations
   // in selector/hook arguments that would otherwise trigger infinite
   // re-render loops via useSyncExternalStore / useEffect dep instability
   // (same bug class as #210 — see #212).
   const EMPTY_ACTIVE_WORK: Record<string, WorkContext> = {};
   const EMPTY_RECENT_ISSUES: Array<{ number: number; title?: string; completedAt: string }> = [];
   ```
2. Line 152: change `useStaleWorkDetection(state?.activeWork ?? {}, stalenessThresholdHours)` → `useStaleWorkDetection(state?.activeWork ?? EMPTY_ACTIVE_WORK, stalenessThresholdHours)`.
3. Line 246 (inside `loadState`, default-cwd branch): change `useTikiStateStore.getState().setRecentIssues(currentState?.history?.recentIssues || [])` → `useTikiStateStore.getState().setRecentIssues(currentState?.history?.recentIssues ?? EMPTY_RECENT_ISSUES)`.
4. Line 357 (inside the file-changed listener): same substitution as #3 — use `?? EMPTY_RECENT_ISSUES`.
5. (Likely there are two more occurrences of `setRecentIssues(... || [])` in App.tsx based on the loadState path being two-branched — search the whole file for `recentIssues || []` and `recentIssues ?? []` and replace all of them with the stable constant.)

### Part B — ErrorBoundary component

1. Create `apps/desktop/src/components/ui/ErrorBoundary.tsx`:
   - Class component (React error boundaries require class syntax; functional components cannot do `componentDidCatch`).
   - Accepts `children: ReactNode` and an optional `fallback?: (error: Error, reset: () => void) => ReactNode` for customization. Default fallback renders an inline recoverable error UI: error name + message + a "Reload panel" button that resets the boundary's state.
   - Implements `static getDerivedStateFromError(error)` and `componentDidCatch(error, info)`. The latter `console.error`s the error + info so dev sessions see it.
   - The reset function clears the boundary's error state so children re-mount.
   - Style minimally — inline styles or one small CSS class are fine. No animations needed. Match dark-theme colors used elsewhere in the app (look at existing components for the tokens).

2. Wire it into `apps/desktop/src/App.tsx`:
   - Import the new `ErrorBoundary`.
   - Wrap the center pane (the panel-group region containing KanbanBoard / DependencyGraph / TerminalPane / detail panels) with `<ErrorBoundary>`. The exact wrap location depends on the JSX structure — find the section where these are rendered and place the boundary as a parent so any of them crashing falls back to the recoverable UI without unmounting the sidebar or the top bar.
   - Optional but nice: wrap the sidebar sections too (each in its own boundary so one section's crash doesn't take down the others). Conservative scope here: just the center pane is required for SC4.

### Part C — Smoke verification

1. Run `pnpm build` from repo root — must complete without errors.
2. Run `pnpm typecheck` — must be clean.
3. Run `pnpm test` — existing tests must continue passing (65 shared + 122 desktop + 28 Rust per recent baseline, give or take if test counts have moved).
4. The actual runtime verification (KanbanBoard renders without infinite loop) requires a live desktop dev session, which a sub-agent cannot drive. The build + typecheck + test pass is the strongest automated signal we have; manual runtime verification by the user comes after ship.

Do NOT add a grep/eslint regression check in this phase (SC from the issue body suggests it but it's scope creep for a hotfix). File a follow-up issue for that hardening if desired.
verification: App.tsx contains module-level `EMPTY_ACTIVE_WORK` and `EMPTY_RECENT_ISSUES` constants (grep finds the literal names)
`grep -n 'recentIssues \?\? \[\]\|recentIssues || \[\]' apps/desktop/src/App.tsx` returns ZERO matches (all replaced with the stable constant)
`apps/desktop/src/components/ui/ErrorBoundary.tsx` exists, is a class component, exports `ErrorBoundary` as a named export, and contains `static getDerivedStateFromError` AND `componentDidCatch`
`apps/desktop/src/App.tsx` imports `ErrorBoundary` and uses it as a wrapper around the panel-group region (verify by grepping for `<ErrorBoundary` in App.tsx — must return ≥ 1 match)
`pnpm build` from repo root exits 0 (no build errors)
`pnpm test` from repo root exits 0 (existing test counts maintained or grown)
